desc: Design Ventilation System Command: Imports MEP systems from a MEPBridge JSON file with Neural Loading visualization.
summary: Design Ventilation System Command: Imports MEP systems from a MEPBridge JSON file with Neural Loading visualization. User selects TWO inputs: 1. JSON file path 2. Base point for placement. Uses silent Excel auto-mapping for blocks (no manual mapping UI). Creates ducts, pipes, cable trays, fittings (elbows, tees, transitions), and family instances.
usecase: User wants to design the ventilation system from AutoCAD JSON export. User wants to import MEP ductwork and equipment from coordinated model. User wants automated ventilation routing from a coordinated JSON model. User says "design the ventilation" or "create ventilation system".
notfor: Not for autonomous AI execution. This command requires interactive Revit UI input (dialogs, picks, or manual confirmation).
precondition: Active document must be open. User must have MEPBridge JSON file. At least one level must exist in the model. Appropriate MEP types (duct, pipe, cable tray) must be loaded in the model.
postcondition: MEP elements (ducts, pipes, trays), fittings (elbows, tees, transitions), and blocks are created. Log file is written next to the JSON file with .ventilation.log.txt extension. Neural Loading dialog shows completion summary.
logictree:
check: jsonPath.isValid → proceed
check: basePoint.isSelected → proceed
check: dto.Edges.count > 0 → proceed
resolve: excelMapper.exists → autoMap silently
keywords: can you please design the ventilation

selection_inputs.name: BasePoint
selection_inputs.prompt: Pick Revit basepoint (target origin for import)

outputs.name: DuctsCreated
outputs.description: Number of MEP curve segments created

preprompts: Preparing to create your ventilation ductwork...
successprompts: Ventilation system created successfully! {DuctsCreated} segments, {ElbowsPlaced} elbows, {TeesPlaced} tees placed.
Done! Your ventilation system is ready with {DuctsCreated} MEP curves.
Ventilation design complete! Created {DuctsCreated} ducts with {ElbowsPlaced} fittings.
